articles.headline: OpenAI Commits $20 Billion to Cerebras Chips Over Three Years
articles.summary: OpenAI has agreed to spend over $20 billion on Cerebras-powered server capacity, roughly double its previous chipmaker commitment, with potential minority stake warrants. The deal signals a strategic shift in AI competition from model development to long-term control of infrastructure, chips, and servers—a critical factor affecting compute costs and AI deployment timelines for enterprises.

articles.headline: Data Center Demand Surges 16% Annually Through 2028 as AI Workloads Drive Gigawatt-Scale Infrastructure Shift
articles.summary: International demand for data centers and computing power is expected to increase 16% annually through 2028, with many sites reaching gigawatt-scale capacity. Energy consumption and power availability have become central planning constraints, driving up electricity costs in concentrated regions and forcing developers to explore on-site power generation—a major cost consideration for businesses planning AI deployments.

articles.headline: Enterprises Move from AI Pilots to Production with Managed Services, Expanding Software and Cloud Spend
articles.summary: Internet Initiative reports that enterprises are transitioning AI projects from pilot phase to production deployments using managed AI services. This shift signals accelerating software spend and cloud adoption among businesses, with practical implications for IT budgets, vendor selection, and competitive positioning for companies offering managed AI infrastructure.
